selectionPolicy: Provide style-language choices, not templates. The author should pick one profile per section when it helps avoid sameness, then write freeform Tailwind HTML within runtime guardrails.

profiles.visualLanguage: editorial magazine spread with asymmetric copy, calm whitespace, and a strong visual anchor
typographyMoves: small eyebrow or label only when useful
colorMoves: warm off-white or pale gray base
profiles.motionIntent: soft reveal or carousel handoff only if a registered interaction exists
assetFit: icon family only for small support cues

profiles.visualLanguage: high-utility dashboard with grouped actions, scan-first cards, and clear state affordances
layoutMoves: make one primary path visually dominant
typographyMoves: small metadata rows for status or count
profiles.motionIntent: drawer, tabs, segmented controls, or accordion when registered interactions are available

profiles.visualLanguage: commerce-focused purchase rail with persistent action clarity and product confidence cues
layoutMoves: separate reassurance cues from primary CTA
use sticky affordance only when useful
keep product media dominant but not noisy
profiles.motionIntent: sticky buybox, accordion, or gallery carousel when registered interactions are available
avoid: decorative icons that look like promo badges

profiles.visualLanguage: cinematic product stage with strong contrast, layered depth, and one decisive product moment
layoutMoves: copy block placed in safe area
colorMoves: deep charcoal, black, or muted gradient
profiles.motionIntent: carousel or compare slider only when registered and relevant
avoid: small product thumbnails as hero backgrounds

profiles.visualLanguage: soft service hub with reassuring cards, rounded geometry, and guided next steps
layoutMoves: one lead service card plus compact supporting cards
colorMoves: soft ivory, pale blue, or mist gray
profiles.motionIntent: accordion, tabs, or modal when registered and necessary
avoid: generic equal cards with no lead path

profiles.visualLanguage: mobile-first touch stack with thumb-zone actions, strong vertical rhythm, and compact state feedback
layoutMoves: bottom action or drawer when useful
profiles.motionIntent: drawer, sticky, tabs, or segmented control when registered interactions are available
